decks.notes: This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures and then bringing them back onto the battlefield for a devastating combo. By using cards like Faithless Looting, Thought Scour, and Unearth, the deck can efficiently get key creatures like Emperor of Bones and Archon of Cruelty into play. With the ability to disrupt opponent's strategies using cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and secure a victory through its reanimation combo.

This aggro-blink deck focuses on quickly deploying creatures like Witch Enchanter, Ranger-Captain of Eos, and Guide of Souls to apply pressure on the opponent. Utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Solitude for protection and removal, the deck aims to control the board while still maintaining an aggressive strategy. Additionally, the deck includes powerful blink targets such as Haliya, Guided by Light and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to generate advantage and overwhelm the opponent. By combining aggressive creatures with blink effects, this deck aims to out-value and outpace the opponent in Modern 1 vs 1 matchups.

The main concept of this deck is to use a variety of spells and abilities to manipulate the opponent's resources and disrupt their game plan while assembling a combo finish with Goblin Charbelcher or Hydroelectric Specimen. By utilizing counterspells, bounce effects, and card draw, the deck aims to control the game until it can win through a powerful combo. The deck also includes ways to search for and tutor key combo pieces like Lotus Bloom and Whir of Invention, allowing for consistent execution of the combo strategy.

The deck focuses on cycling creatures and spells to fill the graveyard with powerful creatures, then casting Living End to bring them all back into play. Shardless Agent helps dig for key pieces, while cards like Force of Negation provide protection. The deck also includes various lands to support the strategy, such as Undercity Sewers for graveyard interaction and Boseiju, Who Endures for uncounterable spells. With strong creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Endurance, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a board full of threats after casting Living End.

This Boros Aggro deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of fast creatures and burn spells. It utilizes cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer to put pressure on the opponent early in the game, while also using burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to finish off opponents or clear blockers. The deck also includes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to provide additional support for its creatures. The strategy is to maintain aggressive board presence and keep the opponent on the back foot.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with combo pieces and then use reanimation spells such as Persist and Unearth to bring back powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones. Cards like Thoughtseize and Inquisition of Kozilek help disrupt the opponent's plans, while Faithless Looting and Psychic Frog provide card advantage. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with big creatures and efficient removal spells, creating a powerful combo-oriented reanimator strategy in the Modern 1 vs 1 format.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game using powerful spells like Stock Up, Kozilek's Command, and Force of Negation while ramping up mana to cast big threats like Ugin, Emrakul, and Otawara. The deck revolves around assembling UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Urza's Tower, Urza's Power Plant) to generate a large amount of mana quickly. By controlling the game with counterspells and removal, the deck aims to play big threats ahead of schedule and overwhelm the opponent with its powerful cards and tight resource management.

This UW Control deck focuses on maintaining board control through counterspells and removal while generating card advantage with Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Consult the Star Charts. It also utilizes Isochron Scepter for repeated spell interactions and the ability to lock down opponents with Orim's Chant. The deck includes powerful finishers like The Wandering Emperor and March of Otherworldly Light to close out games. With a solid mana base and versatile sideboard companion in Kaheera, the Orphanguard, this deck aims to outlast opponents and secure victory through strategic decision-making and resource management.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize a combo strategy that does not rely on traditional land cards. Instead, the focus is on cards like Lotus Bloom and Hydroelectric Specimen to generate mana and card advantage. The deck includes a mix of counterspells, disruption, and card draw to control the game while setting up key combo pieces like Goblin Charbelcher. The goal is to efficiently assemble the combo pieces and win the game without needing a traditional mana base, making it a unique and unpredictable archetype in Modern 1 vs 1 play.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on fast and aggressive plays, utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Goblin Bombardment to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It also includes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory for added versatility and power. The deck aims to play creatures and spells quickly to deal damage early and maintain pressure on the opponent. With a mix of low-cost creatures and powerful finishers, this deck aims to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by countering opponent's spells, removing threats, and manipulating the board state. It utilizes Teferi, Time Raveler to control the tempo of the game and Isochron Scepter to repeat powerful spells like Orim's Chant. The deck also includes Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion for additional support. The mana base is designed to provide access to the necessary colors for spells and includes powerful lands like Hall of Storm Giants. Overall, the strategy is to outlast the opponent and win through attrition.

The deck focuses on quickly and aggressively deploying artifacts, leveraging cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga to create a board presence while using cheap removal and counterspells to control the opponent's actions. By efficiently generating mana through artifacts and utilizing strong synergies with cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Engineered Explosives, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of evasive threats and disruptive spells, all while maintaining card advantage and flexibility.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to play efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, while using cards like Galvanic Discharge and Lightning Bolt for removal and direct damage. The deck also includes ways to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Thraben Charm. By maintaining pressure on the opponent and controlling the board, this deck aims to win the game swiftly.

This deck focuses on assembling a toolbox of creatures to create powerful combos using cards like Yawgmoth, Thran Physician and Green Sun's Zenith. It utilizes various creatures with unique abilities like Young Wolf, Strangleroot Geist, and Walking Ballista to control the board and generate value. The deck also includes cards like Delighted Halfling and Agatha's Soul Cauldron to maintain card advantage and outvalue the opponent. With a mix of utility creatures, efficient removal, and card draw, this deck aims to outmaneuver the opponent and win through combo interactions.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and sacrificing them to generate mana and power up creatures quickly. It also includes disruptive elements to control the opponent's board and disrupt their strategies. By combining aggressive plays with efficient mana generation, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and powerful board presence. Additionally, the deck incorporates synergies between artifacts to create strong combos and maximize its effectiveness in combat. It relies on a mix of creature aggression and artifact manipulation to achieve victory in a quick and dynamic manner.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through land destruction and removal spells, while also utilizing creatures with flash and powerful abilities to maintain board presence. By disrupting the opponent's mana base and removing key threats, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and eventually secure a victory with a combination of removal, creature beats, and powerful sorceries. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion provides additional flexibility and synergy with the deck's creature choices. Ultimately, the deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver its opponents to secure a win.
